why_it_matters: DORA's low-latency, composable, and distributed dataflow capabilities are directly applicable to building a unique real estate tech stack. For VR tours and 3D maps, it can orchestrate real-time data streams from spatial sensors or user interactions, enabling highly responsive and immersive experiences. For the AI voice caller and WhatsApp bot, it can manage complex conversational flows and integrate various AI models (NLP, TTS, ASR) with minimal latency, creating a more natural and effective communication experience than competitors. The 'pipelines' concept allows for sophisticated, multi-modal AI agents that can process spatial data, voice commands, and text inputs simultaneously, leading to a breakthrough in interactive property exploration and sales engagement.

why_it_matters: This paper directly addresses a critical challenge in text-to-3D generation, which is fundamental for creating and manipulating 3D content for VR tours and 3D maps. Overcoming the 'collapse' of text-driven inversion for out-of-distribution shapes would enable the creation of highly diverse and unique property representations from natural language, a capability no other real estate tech company currently possesses at scale or quality. It directly impacts the ability to generate novel, high-fidelity 3D assets for properties that might not fit standard templates, offering a significant competitive advantage in visual property presentation and customization.

title: Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report

Representing a significant evolution over its predecessor, Qwen3.5-Omni scales to hundreds of billions of parameters and supports a 256k context length. By leveraging a massive dataset comprising heterogeneous text-vision pairs and over 100 million hours of audio-visual content, the model demonstrates robust omni-modality capabilities. Qwen3.5-Omni-plus achieves SOTA results across 215 audio and audio-vi
why_it_matters: Qwen3.5-Omni's robust omni-modality, especially its audio-visual capabilities and massive context length, directly enables a breakthrough in 3D/voice/spatial experiences for property sales. title: ArtifactNet: Detecting AI-Generated Music via Forensic Residual Physics
summary: [1 comments] We present ArtifactNet, a lightweight framework that detects AI-generated music by reframing the problem as forensic physics -- extracting and analyzing the physical artifacts that neural audio codecs inevitably imprint on generated audio. title: Target-Oriented Pretraining Data Selection via Neuron-Activated Graph

title: Trajectory as the Teacher: Few-Step Discrete Flow Matching via Energy-Navigated Distillation
summary: [1 comments] Discrete flow matching generates text by iteratively transforming noise tokens into coherent language, but may require hundreds of forward passes. title: MatryoshkaLoRA: Learning Accurate Hierarchical Low-Rank Representations for LLM Fine-Tuning

title: Key-Value Means

title: A Closed-Form Upper Bound for Admissible Learning-Rate Steps in Belief-Space Dynamics
summary: [1 comments] Learning-rate steps are usually treated as hyperparameters. This limitation is especially pronounced in tasks where generated videos must satisfy explicit spatial, temporal, or logical constraints. title: PEEK: Context Map as an Orientation Cache for Long-Context LLM Agents
summary: [1 comments] Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly operate over long and recurring external contexts, like document corpora and code repositories. title: ESI-Bench: Towards Embodied Spatial Intelligence that Closes the Perception-Action Loop
summary: [1 comments] Spatial intelligence unfolds through a perception-action loop: agents act to acquire observations, and reason about how observations vary as a function of action. title: Code-Guided Reasoning for Small Language Models: Evaluating Executable MCQA Scaffolds

title: Semantic Generative Tuning for Unified Multimodal Models
summary: [1 comments] Unified multimodal models (UMMs) strive to consolidate visual understanding and visual generation within a single architecture. summary: [1 comments] We present GoLongRL, a fully open-source, capability-oriented post-training recipe for long-context reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). title: IndusAgent: Reinforcing Open-Vocabulary Industrial Anomaly Detection with Agentic Tools
summary: [1 comments] Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown remarkable capability in bridging visual perception and textual reasoning, enabling zero-shot understanding across diverse industrial scenarios. summary: [1 comments] Safety post-training can improve the harmfulness and policy compliance of Large Language Models (LLMs), but it may also reduce general utility, a phenomenon often described as the alignment tax. title: OcclusionFormer: Arranging Z-Order for Layout-Grounded Image Generation
summary: [1 comments] Recent layout-to-image models have achieved remarkable progress in spatial controllability. However, they still struggle with inter-object occlusion. title: Conditional Equivalence of DPO and RLHF: Implicit Assumption, Failure Modes, and Provable Alignment
summary: [1 comments] Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has emerged as a popular alternative to Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), offering theoretical equivalence with simpler implementation. summary: [1 comments] Video Virtual Try-On (VVT) aims to seamlessly replace a garment on a person in a video with a new one. title: SG-OPD: Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation via Sign-Consistency Gating and Phased Teacher Sampling
summary: [1 comments] On-policy distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own trajectories with dense per-token supervision from a stronger teacher, and often outperforms off-policy distillation and standard reinforcement learning. However, we find that its effectiveness implicitly relies on two assumptions that frequently break in practice: trajectory-level alignment between the student and the teacher, and uniform token-level reliability of the teacher's preferences. title: Robust-U1: Can MLLMs Self-Recover Corrupted Visual Content for Robust Understanding?
summary: [1 comments] Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable success in visual understanding, yet their performance degrades significantly under real-world visual corruptions. While existing robustness enhancement approaches exist, they are limited: black-box feature alignment lacks interpretability, and white-box text-based reasoning cannot restore lost pixel-level details. This work investigates a fundamental research question: Can MLLMs recover corrupted visual content by themselves?

title: The Cold-Start Safety Gap in LLM Agents
summary: [1 comments] Are tool-calling LLM agents equally safe throughout a conversation? We discover they are not: agents are most vulnerable at the very start of a session and become substantially safer after a few regular agentic tasks -- a phenomenon we term the cold-start safety gap.
